name: Integral de la Cabra Trail
name_original: Parque Regional El Valle y Carrascoy — Circular desde Murcia (PR-MU 21 & 23)
region: Zepa Monte El Valle Y Sierras De Altaona Y Escalona
description: Hike the difficult 19.8-mile Integral de la Cabra Trail in Murcia, gaining 3333 feet with panoramic views from Pico del Águila.
region_intro: Urban hiking trails in Zepa Monte El Valle Y Sierras De Altaona Y Escalona offer diverse experiences, primarily focusing on the flatter, accessible areas within and around urban centers. The region features a blend of natural riverine landscapes, such as the Segura River, and cultivated green spaces like botanical gardens. While the broader ZEPA encompasses mountainous terrain and Mediterranean forests, the urban routes typically navigate cityscapes and riverside paths. These trails provide opportunities to explore the natural beauty intertwined with the urban environment. Best urban hiking trails in Zepa Monte El Valle Y Sierras De Altaona Y Escalona The most popular urban hiking route is Murcia Río Riverside Walk – Murcia Town Hall loop from Estadio de La Condomina, a 5.1 miles (8.1 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route follows the Segura River, offering views of the city's riverside architecture. Another top favourite among local hikers is Murcia Cathedral – Segura River Walk loop from Murcia, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path. This trail combines historical city landmarks with pleasant riverside sections. Local hikers also love the Malecón Botanical Garden – Murcia Río Riverside Walk loop from Murcia, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through botanical gardens and along the river, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Hiking in Zepa Monte El Valle Y Sierras De Altaona Y Escalona is defined by riverside paths, urban green spaces, and city center exploration. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate urban hikes. The routes in Zepa Monte El Valle Y Sierras De Altaona Y Escalona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 400 reviews. More than 2500 hikers have used komoot to explore Zepa Monte El Valle Y Sierras De Altaona Y Escalona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many urban hiking trails are available in Zepa Monte El Valle y Sierras de Altaona y Escalona?
A: There are over 45 urban hiking trails in Zepa Monte El Valle y Sierras de Altaona y Escalona, offering a variety of options for exploring the region's urban and natural landscapes.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of urban hikes in this region?
A: The urban hikes in Zepa Monte El Valle y Sierras de Altaona y Escalona are generally easy to moderate. Approximately 24 routes are considered easy, while 22 are moderate, making them accessible for most fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular urban walks in Zepa Monte El Valle y Sierras de Altaona y Escalona?
A: Yes, many urban routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33771732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murcia Río Riverside Walk – Murcia Town Hall loop from Estadio de La Condomina&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33756763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malecón Botanical Garden – Murcia Río Riverside Walk loop from Murcia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on urban hikes here?
A: Urban hikes in this region often blend cityscapes with natural elements. You can expect to see Mediterranean forests, agricultural land, and the winding Segura River. Some trails also offer views of semi-arid habitats and rocky outcrops.

Q: Are there urban hikes that pass by historical or cultural landmarks?
A: Yes, several urban hikes incorporate historical and cultural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10323880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murcia Cathedral – Segura River Walk loop from Murcia&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the iconic Murcia Cathedral. Other routes might pass by the Murcia Town Hall or offer glimpses of the region's historical architecture.

Q: What natural features can I see on urban trails in Zepa Monte El Valle y Sierras de Altaona y Escalona?
A: While primarily urban, trails often skirt natural areas. You might encounter the Segura River, various Mediterranean flora like Aleppo pine and rosemary, and potentially catch distant views of peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Relojero Peak&lt;/a&gt; or unique formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Mamellones Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the urban hiking trails in Zepa Monte El Valle y Sierras de Altaona y Escalona accessible by public transport?
A: Many urban trailheads, especially those within Murcia city, are well-served by public transport, making it convenient to reach starting points without a car. It's advisable to check local bus routes for specific access points.

Q: Is parking available near the urban trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in urban areas near trailheads, particularly in and around Murcia city. Depending on the specific starting point, you may find street parking or public car parks.

Q: What should I bring for an urban hike in this region?
A: For urban hikes, comfortable walking shoes are essential. Depending on the season, bring sun protection (hat, sunscreen) and plenty of water. A light jacket might be useful for cooler evenings or shaded areas. Don't forget your phone for navigation and photos.

Q: Are there family-friendly urban hikes in Zepa Monte El Valle y Sierras de Altaona y Escalona?
A: Yes, many of the easy and moderate urban trails are suitable for families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31687541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murcia Town Hall – Segura River Walk loop from Murcia&lt;/a&gt; are relatively short and flat, offering an enjoyable experience for all ages.

Q: Are dogs allowed on urban hiking trails in Zepa Monte El Valle y Sierras de Altaona y Escalona?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on urban trails, especially in public parks and riverside paths, provided they are kept on a leash. Always check local regulations for specific areas or parks you plan to visit.

Q: What is the best season for urban hikes in this region?
A: Spring and autumn are ideal for urban hiking in Zepa Monte El Valle y Sierras de Altaona y Escalona, offering pleasant temperatures. Winter can also be enjoyable with mild weather. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late evening hikes are recommended during this period.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Zepa Monte El Valle y Sierras de Altaona y Escalona?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the accessibility of the trails, the blend of urban exploration with natural riverine landscapes, and the opportunity to discover local landmarks.
tips: One of the many peaks within the El Valle and Carrascoy Regional Park. From its 623 meters above sea level you can appreciate very nice views of the entire park. You will have a demanding but picturesque trail going up and you can take one of the variants to descend if you prefer. Depending on the path you choose, there will be more loose stone, so be careful.

La Asomada castle is located in the municipality of Murcia, nestled in a strategic location: a large mountainous elevation known as Cabezo del Puerto that rises 532 m above sea level, at whose feet is the Puerto de la Cadena , one of the mountain passes that, since ancient times, connect the Campo de Cartagena and the Huerta de Murcia.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Integral de la Cabra Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail features varied and demanding terrain. You can expect mountainous sections with significant climbs and descents, rugged paths, and areas with rock formations. Some parts may feel like a 'goat path' due to their challenging nature.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers impressive and diverse scenery with breathtaking panoramic views. Key highlights include Pico del Águila and the View from Castillo de la Asomada, providing expansive vistas of deep valleys and the surrounding natural park.

faqs.question: Is the Integral de la Cabra Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as difficult and requires a good to very good level of fitness. With over 1,000 meters of elevation gain and a distance of nearly 32 kilometers, it presents a significant physical challenge and is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail generally depends on avoiding extreme heat or cold. Spring and autumn typically offer more moderate temperatures, making the demanding climbs more manageable. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Integral de la Cabra Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead is not provided in the tour data. However, as the trail is located within the Parque Regional de El Valle y Carrascoy, it's advisable to research designated parking areas near the park's main access points or trailheads for the PR-MU 21/23 route.

faqs.question: Is the Integral de la Cabra Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in natural parks allow dogs, specific regulations for the Integral de la Cabra Trail or the Parque Regional de El Valle y Carrascoy regarding dogs (e.g., leash requirements, restricted areas) are not detailed. It's best to check with the park authorities directly before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Parque Regional de El Valle y Carrascoy?
faqs.answer: Generally, regional parks in Spain, including the Parque Regional de El Valle y Carrascoy, do not require permits or charge entrance fees for hiking. However, specific activities or access to certain protected areas within the park might have regulations. It's always a good idea to check the official park website for any updates on rules or restrictions.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other official trails. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the 'Integral de la Cabra 2015' (61%) and 'Antonio Pérez Nortes' (41%). You'll also find smaller segments of paths like 'Veteranos 92', 'Camino de Santiago del Azahar', and 'Camino del Apóstol' along the way.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail traverses a natural park, you may encounter various local wildlife. While specific species are not listed, typical Mediterranean forest fauna could include birds of prey, small mammals, and reptiles. Maintaining a respectful distance and not disturbing animals is always recommended.

faqs.question: Is the Integral de la Cabra Trail a loop or point-to-point?
faqs.answer: Based on the tour data, the 'Integral de la Cabra Trail' is described as a 'circular' route, indicating it is a loop trail. This means you will generally finish at or near your starting point.

faqs.question: What should I pack for hiking this difficult trail?
faqs.answer: Given its difficulty and length, you should pack essentials like sufficient water (there's no mention of water sources on the trail), high-energy snacks, appropriate hiking footwear with good ankle support, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), layers of clothing, a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone. A map or GPS device is also highly recommended.
